title: Angry Andy

jokes.text: Phyllis says the same 12 cliches every time it rains. So... I promised everyone that if she says them all by noon today, I will send out for hot chocolates.
jokes.setup: Jim reveals he's tracking Phyllis's predictable rain clichés
jokes.punchline: The office bet and reward system around her predictability
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank setup with office-wide participation. Well-structured observational humor about workplace familiarity.

jokes.text: So how's the drive in? Oh, nobody knows how to drive in the rain. You don't say? Yeah, you know the roads are actually the slickest in the first half hour? Oh, the plants are gonna love this.
jokes.setup: Jim is deliberately prompting Phyllis to say her clichés
jokes.punchline: She delivers them exactly as predicted while Jim plays along
jokes.explanation: Solid execution of the setup. Jim's fake encouragement and Phyllis's obliviousness create good character-based humor.

jokes.text: This rain... Does it make you wanna be doing something? What do you mean? You know, like aren't some things just so nice and cozy in the rain?
jokes.setup: Jim desperately trying to get Phyllis to say the final cliché
jokes.punchline: His increasingly awkward and leading questions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jim's desperation as time runs out. The awkwardness of his leading questions is funny.

jokes.text: And that's noon. Exactly. I mean, normally the rain would make me want to stay at home, curled up with a good book. But everybody's being so nice to me today.
jokes.setup: The whole office manipulation to get her to say the final cliché
jokes.punchline: She delivers it perfectly while noting how nice everyone is being
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. The irony of Phyllis noting everyone's kindness while being manipulated is excellent.

jokes.text: She's sweet. I just wish there was pockets.
jokes.setup: Kevin commenting on a sweater Nellie gave him
jokes.punchline: His simple, practical complaint delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin - simple, literal thinking delivered with perfect economy. Very in character.

jokes.text: What happened to Old Salty? Nellie let me bobblize him. His name is now Captain Mutato.
jokes.setup: Question about Kevin's old bobblehead
jokes.punchline: Kevin's made-up word 'bobblize' and the absurd new name
jokes.explanation: 'Bobblize' is a great Kevin neologism. The absurd superhero name fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: I've written quite a bit of X-Men fan fiction. Captain Mutato is half man, half mermaid. So he can fight crime as a man, and make love as a mermaid.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his X-Men fan fiction character
jokes.punchline: The bizarre half-man/half-mermaid concept with specific abilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and perfectly Kevin. The specific division of man/mermaid abilities is wonderfully weird.

jokes.text: Most of my writing involves the latter.
jokes.setup: Kevin just explained Captain Mutato's dual abilities
jokes.punchline: Deadpan admission that he mostly writes about the mermaid love-making
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. The formal language 'the latter' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I am the famous Andy Bernard you've been hearing all about.
jokes.setup: Andy introducing himself to Nellie with false confidence
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-importance while having no actual power
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Andy's oblivious arrogance, but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: No. Obviously, we'll figure out the, uh, logistics of moving all of this stuff out of here. But... You know, the sooner the better. Get back to normalcy. Hmm, no.
jokes.setup: Andy politely asking for his office back
jokes.punchline: Nellie's repeated flat 'no' responses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation through repetition. Nellie's blunt refusal contrasts well with Andy's politeness.

jokes.text: And then just lay him in his crib, and then bicycle his legs. And then after Jim quiets down, you do the same thing with your baby.
jokes.setup: Ryan giving parenting advice about putting a baby to bed
jokes.punchline: Implies Jim is also a baby who needs to be put down first
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection joke. The structure works well and the implication is clear and funny.

jokes.text: Because Kelly is Indian, and... Oh, that's it. Race had nothing to do with it. I just knew they'd be good together. Why!
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining why he recommended Kelly for his pediatrician
jokes.punchline: His immediate backtrack when he realizes how it sounds, then the helpless 'Why!'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Ryan's self-aware panic and the final 'Why!' work well for his character.

jokes.text: Sorry, just to be clear, you're saying do not shake the baby. Don't shake the baby. Um, a lot times, parents get frustrated 'cause the baby's crying and they shake the baby. And you gotta-- You can't do that.
jokes.setup: Ryan giving very basic parenting advice about not shaking babies
jokes.punchline: Jim's incredulous clarification and Ryan's over-explanation of obvious advice
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of absurd over-explanation of the most basic parenting rule. Jim's reactions sell it.

jokes.text: Kelly and I broke up and she can do whatever she wants. And her new boyfriend seems awesome... If you're into Indian people. I'm not.
jokes.setup: Ryan claiming to be mature about Kelly's new relationship
jokes.punchline: His casual racism delivered as personal preference
jokes.explanation: Very in character for Ryan's casual awfulness. The delivery as personal taste makes it darkly funny.

jokes.text: Maybe we weren't right together, but... it's weird. I'd rather she be alone than with somebody. Is that love?
jokes.setup: Ryan reflecting on his feelings about Kelly's new relationship
jokes.punchline: His selfish possessiveness presented as a genuine question about love
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Ryan's toxic possessiveness framed as philosophical inquiry is perfectly him.

jokes.text: That's about it, yeah.
jokes.setup: Ryan asking if his selfish possessiveness is love
jokes.punchline: The interviewer's blunt confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. The economy is exceptional - three words that land the punchline.

jokes.text: Oh, Bobby, get accustomed to it, because then it feels good when they finally say yes.
jokes.setup: Robert saying he's not accustomed to people saying no
jokes.punchline: Nellie's inappropriate sexual advice/philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Nellie's inappropriate boundary-crossing. The sexual undertone is clear.

jokes.text: I never allow sexual desire to influence a business decision. So I find it best to... recuse myself temporarily until I've had a chance to make love, and then go back and... analyze the situation rationally.
jokes.setup: Robert explaining his business decision-making process
jokes.punchline: His bizarre method of addressing sexual desire before making choices
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Robert California moment. His pseudo-philosophical approach to horniness is hilarious.

jokes.text: Buffett operates the same way.
jokes.setup: Robert's bizarre business philosophy about sexual desire
jokes.punchline: Claiming Warren Buffett uses the same method
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. The deadpan delivery of this absurd claim is gold.

jokes.text: I'm going to dock your pay $100. Okay. Five... four... You can't dock my pay. Angela! Dock Andy's pay $100! On it! Great!
jokes.setup: Andy and Nellie having a standoff over office authority
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous back-and-forth pay docking with Angela just going along
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the power struggle. Angela's enthusiastic compliance adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: I got a little bit of an anger problem. Got me in some trouble a couple years ago. When I see him start to get mad, I just... Put my hand on his arm like this.
jokes.setup: Darryl explaining his anger management technique
jokes.punchline: Reference to his past anger issues and his gentle calming method
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Darryl's character development. The gentle approach contrasts his past issues.

jokes.text: $10,000. 5...4... You're just saying numbers. It's meaningless. It's literally like... 3... 2... One.
jokes.setup: The escalating pay docking war
jokes.punchline: Andy jumping to an absurd amount and Nellie pointing out the meaninglessness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurdity of their power struggle. Nellie's observation is apt.

jokes.text: Hey. So last night was so not a big deal. Oh, yeah, I was just tired. We both were. Yeah. Plus, I was definitely not my normal sexy self.
jokes.setup: Erin and Andy awkwardly discussing their sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Their over-compensation and Erin's attempt at sexiness
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward relationship humor. The over-explanation creates appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: Yeah. I know it's not... a big deal.
jokes.setup: Erin insisting their sexual problem isn't a big deal
jokes.punchline: Andy's pause and emphasis suggesting it IS a big deal to him
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing Andy's insecurity through delivery rather than words.

jokes.text: Oh, those are from our weekend at the Timeshare. Yeah, the fractional ownership property.
jokes.setup: Looking at photos from their trip
jokes.punchline: Andy's pretentious correction of 'timeshare' to 'fractional ownership property'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's need to sound sophisticated about mundane things.

jokes.text: I'm in love with Kelly Kapoor. And I don't know how I'm gonna feel tomorrow or the next day or the day after that, but I do know that right here, right now, all I can think about is spending the rest of my life with her. Again, that could change.
jokes.setup: Ryan's passionate declaration of love for Kelly
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with 'Again, that could change'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan moment. The romantic buildup completely deflated by his casual disclaimer is brilliantly in character.

jokes.text: When you lost the manager job... Yes? Did it affect you outside of the office? How? I don't know what the technical term is. Hmm. Penial... softiosis.
jokes.setup: Interviewer asking about Andy's job loss affecting his personal life
jokes.punchline: Andy's made-up medical term 'penial softiosis' for erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Great neologism that sounds almost medical. Andy's awkward attempt at clinical language is funny.

jokes.text: Washington Monument. Okay. Eiffel Tower. Okay, okay.
jokes.setup: Andy apparently demonstrating his erectile function
jokes.punchline: References to increasingly tall, phallic monuments
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual metaphor. The escalation from Washington Monument to Eiffel Tower works.

jokes.text: He's so sweet. He pointed to my latte and he said, 'Kelly, that will be the color of our children.'
jokes.setup: Kelly describing Ryan's romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Ryan's creepy racial comment about their future children
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Kelly finding this sweet while it's obviously creepy is perfect for both characters.

jokes.text: Remember how it felt when he cheated on you though? Which time?
jokes.setup: Pam reminding Kelly about Ryan cheating
jokes.punchline: Kelly's casual acknowledgment of multiple cheating incidents
jokes.explanation: Great economy and delivery. Kelly's matter-of-fact response reveals the dysfunction perfectly.

jokes.text: I am not going to let Kelly throw her life away on Ryan. And it has nothing to do with access to my pediatrician. Why you would even ask or were going to ask, because I-- I feel like that question was coming.
jokes.setup: Ryan claiming altruistic motives about Kelly
jokes.punchline: His defensive pre-emption of questions about his selfish motives
jokes.explanation: Good character work. Ryan's defensive spiral reveals his true motives while he denies them.

jokes.text: Oh, and Andy lost his masculinity, so congratulations on that. What do you mean? Erin made it clear to me that he was unable to perform sexually last night. By contrast, I went to sleep with an erection so large it was like I was wearing no blanket at all.
jokes.setup: Dwight reporting Andy's sexual problems to Nellie
jokes.punchline: His vivid description of his own contrasting sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight. The specific visual metaphor about the blanket is brilliantly weird and memorable.

jokes.text: I just wanted to take the man's job, not his manhood.
jokes.setup: Learning about Andy's sexual problems
jokes.punchline: Nellie's faux concern with the job/manhood wordplay
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay and good delivery. The alliteration makes it more memorable.

jokes.text: Andrew, not everyone here is important. And the word is impotence. Which is important in its own way.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to hold a meeting about everyone being important
jokes.punchline: Nellie's correction and wordplay with important/impotence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. The philosophical twist adds depth to the pun.

jokes.text: 'Take a man's job, but leave him his balls.' Margaret Thatcher said that... probably. Don't know. Don't read. Didn't see the movie.
jokes.setup: Nellie making a profound-sounding quote
jokes.punchline: Admitting she doesn't know if it's real and her anti-intellectual stance
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from fake profundity to admission of ignorance. Very Nellie character work.

jokes.text: It's not just a human problem. Flounders frequently experience impotence, especially when converting from male to female and then back again to male.
jokes.setup: Discussion of erectile dysfunction as a human problem
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre fish knowledge applied to the situation
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity. His specific marine biology knowledge applied inappropriately is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm gonna prove it right here and now. What are you doing? What is this? Stop that! Dwight! Dwight, stop that. Stop it.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming he doesn't have erectile dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Him apparently trying to demonstrate physically in the office
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy moment. The group's horrified reactions sell the inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Um... I have other issues. I'm terrible at math. Overweight. You-- You're in great shape. A lot better shape than I am, tell you what.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Jim about erectile dysfunction experiences
jokes.punchline: Jim's awkward deflection to completely different personal issues
jokes.explanation: Good Jim moment. His desperate deflection and self-deprecation to avoid the topic works well.

jokes.text: Erin doesn't even like sex, remember? You said it feels like getting tackled by a skeleton.
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to help Andy feel better
jokes.punchline: Recalling Erin's graphic description of sex with Andy
jokes.explanation: Great callback and vivid imagery. 'Tackled by a skeleton' is a memorably specific description.

jokes.text: Uh, yes, that time that it was very late. Uh, we'd had sex so many times already, I was exhausted. I could barely see. I was very drunk.
jokes.setup: Jim being pressed to share an erectile dysfunction experience
jokes.punchline: His obviously made-up story with increasingly ridiculous details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jim's discomfort. The obviously fake details are funny and very Jim.

jokes.text: If it makes you feel any better, I never had an orgasm until I was 42. And then when I did, it lasted till I was 44. 43 was ju-- I got nothing done.
jokes.setup: Robert offering comfort about sexual problems
jokes.punchline: His impossible two-year orgasm story
jokes.explanation: Peak Robert California absurdity. The impossible timeline and practical concern about productivity is brilliantly weird.

jokes.text: You know, my rectal electro-ejaculator is rated for bovine use only, but I could let you rent it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of prostate stimulation helping erectile dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Dwight's farm equipment solution offered casually
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly Dwight. The specific agricultural terminology and casual rental offer is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Dad, don't think of it as a demotion. Just think of it as a promotion to a lower level.
jokes.setup: Andy on a phone call about his job situation
jokes.punchline: His illogical attempt to spin a demotion positively
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Andy's desperate spin attempts. The logical contradiction is clear and funny.

jokes.text: Small, bigger, biggest. Oh, sexual innuendo. Not intentional. Shut up!
jokes.setup: Nellie describing a date format preference
jokes.punchline: Her recognition and denial of accidental sexual innuendo, then angry outburst
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor. Nellie catching her own accidental innuendo and getting flustered works well.

jokes.text: Our sex life is none of your businesses! And Andy is the manager, not Nellie! Stop protecting me. I'm a man-- I can protect myself.
jokes.setup: Erin defending Andy against office gossip
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately undermining her defense by saying he can protect himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's insecurity. His immediate contradiction of Erin's defense is telling.

jokes.text: Dad, go to hell! I'm taller than you!
jokes.setup: Andy having an emotional breakdown on the phone
jokes.punchline: His childish insult about height to his father
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's regression to childish behavior. The height insult is perfectly petty.

jokes.text: And why don't you take all your stupid memos and your stupid pens... and your dumb caramels? And your stupid face! And your stupid office! Ah! Ow.
jokes.setup: Andy's angry rant at Nellie
jokes.punchline: His childish insults escalating to throwing things and hurting himself
jokes.explanation: Standard tantrum escalation. The physical comedy of him hurting himself adds to the childishness.

jokes.text: He does not like that wall.
jokes.setup: Andy just punched the wall and hurt himself
jokes.punchline: Kevin's matter-of-fact observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin deadpan. The understatement and literal interpretation is classic him.

jokes.text: Maybe we'll get sent to anger management together. That would actually be cool. I'd love for you to meet some of the guys.
jokes.setup: Andy's violent outburst
jokes.punchline: Darryl's enthusiasm about potential anger management reunion
jokes.explanation: Great callback that treats anger management like a social club. Very Darryl character work.

jokes.text: I don't think you're a very good person. And forgive me, but I feel like I've said this to you before. I don't like you very much.
jokes.setup: Ryan asking Pam to say bad things to his face
jokes.punchline: Pam's brutally honest but politely delivered assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pam moment. The polite delivery of harsh honesty is excellent character work.

jokes.text: I've never met Ravi personally, but I'm gonna go ahead and say just having knowing you short while, Brian, that I prefer Ravi. And again, I've never even met the guy.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether Ryan or Ravi is better for Kelly
jokes.punchline: Creed's brutal assessment of Ryan despite not knowing Ravi, plus wrong name
jokes.explanation: Great Creed moment. His logic is sound but his calling Ryan 'Brian' adds perfect Creed confusion.

jokes.text: Our love scares them. It screws up their cookie-cutter world.
jokes.setup: Everyone telling Ryan to leave Kelly alone
jokes.punchline: Ryan's delusional interpretation as romantic persecution
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan delusion. His interpretation of universal dislike as romantic persecution is very in character.

jokes.text: Oh, boo! Boo! Pam. Boo to you! Boo! You are toxic. You are toxic!
jokes.setup: Andy being told he's demoted
jokes.punchline: His childish 'booing' and calling Pam toxic
jokes.explanation: Standard tantrum behavior. The childish booing shows Andy's regression but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Even if the odds are 50/50 that we'll break up within the week, I wanna roll those dice. I love you, Kelly.
jokes.setup: Ryan's romantic proposal to Kelly
jokes.punchline: His unromantic statistical approach to their relationship odds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan moment. Treating love like a business proposition while claiming romance is brilliantly in character.

jokes.text: Andrew, if you say no one more time, you're fired. So... is there anything else you wanna say? No.
jokes.setup: Robert giving Andy one final chance to avoid firing
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately saying 'no' despite the clear warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Andy's self-destructive defiance despite clear consequences is well-executed.

jokes.text: I can't describe it. I just, for the first time in a long time, I actually feel in control. I feel... alive.
jokes.setup: Andy has just been fired for his defiance
jokes.punchline: His claim of feeling in control while having just lost everything
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment. Andy's delusion about being in control while completely self-destructing is perfect.

jokes.text: Kapoor and Kadesperate. He watches... Second line. He is a drifter out to see... It went the Indian Ocean in calms, one speck of white remains and waters called and Kelly grinned.
jokes.setup: Kelly listening to Ryan's love poem
jokes.punchline: The terrible, pretentious poetry with racial elements and bad grammar
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Ryan's terrible poetry with racial undertones is appropriately awful and in character.

jokes.text: Ryan can never know.
jokes.setup: Kelly secretly moved by Ryan's terrible poem
jokes.punchline: Her whispered confession that she was affected despite herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly moment. Her being moved by terrible poetry despite herself shows her romantic nature and their dynamic.

tmdb_overview: Andy returns to discover Nellie already in the manager's chair.
